propositions: The parent-first claim cascade lacks tests for a cross-process/two-clone race where two agents work different children of the same open parent, and its expected semantics must be documented before being asserted.

Same-tracker policy: agent B seeing the parent already in_progress does not cascade and may claim its child independently under its own assignee; parent ownership does not constrain sibling child assignees.
Two-clone offline policy: parent and child status forks resolve independently per ticket via the reducer's UUID tie-break, so a same-child claim's winner may differ from the parent's winner.
A losing agent's already-written parent and/or child claim is left orphaned under the losing assignee, never rolled back or tombstoned, and is signaled by STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED on the affected ticket.

A TOCTOU bug in claim.py decides to cascade the parent from an unlocked stale open read, so under a genuine race the locked parent claim raises ConcurrencyMismatch and the whole child claim aborts with exit 10.
Fix: when the cascaded parent claim fails but the parent is no longer open because a peer progressed it, treat it as benign and proceed to claim the child; only a still-open parent should abort.
